entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species being studied for male sterility characteristics.

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The condition where pollen grains are non-functional or absent.

entities.name: POLLEN GRAIN SHAPE AND SIZE
entities.description: Morphological characteristics of the pollen grains.

entities.name: EXINE SCULPTURE
entities.description: The pattern and structure of the outer wall of pollen grains.

entities.name: INTERNAL STRUCTURE
entities.description: The inner composition and arrangement of pollen grains.

entities.name: SCANNING ELECTRON MICROSCOPY
entities.description: Imaging technique used to observe the surface and structure of pollen.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-GENETIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A form of male sterility controlled by both cytoplasmic and nuclear genes.

entities.name: MAINTAINER LINES
entities.description: Sorghum lines used to maintain the male sterile lines.

entities.name: VZM2
entities.description: A specific sorghum line characterized by its pollen structure.

entities.name: GI
entities.description: A specific sorghum line characterized by its pollen structure.

entities.name: CK 60
entities.description: A specific sorghum line characterized by its pollen structure.

entities.name: M 35-1
entities.description: A specific sorghum line characterized by its pollen structure.

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: Organic compounds found to be deficient in male sterile pollen.

entities.name: STARCH PARTICLES
entities.description: Carbohydrate granules found to be deficient in male sterile pollen.

entities.name: GENETIC STUDIES
entities.description: Studies used to group sorghum lines based on genetic markers.

entities.name: BIOCHEMICAL STUDIES
entities.description: Studies used to group sorghum lines based on biochemical properties.
